/software-engineering/web-development/AP.title: API
/software-engineering/web-development/AP.content:
# API

# API

|||
|--|--|
|A|Application|
|P|Programming|
|I|Interface|

Allows other programs or application to use functionality that you expose:

* Functions
* Protocols
* Objects

---

## Weather Data Example

<!-- element style="margin: 0; font-size: 30pt" -->

Request:

<!-- element style="margin: 0; font-size: 20pt" -->


````http
GET https://www.7timer.info/bin/astro.php
?lon=15.593063
&lat=48.411018
&ac=0
&unit=metric
&output=json
````

<!-- element style="font-size: 12pt; margin: 0" -->

Response:

<!-- element style="margin: 0; font-size: 20pt" -->


````http
HTTP/1.1 200 OK
Content-Type: application/json

{
  "product": "civil",
	"init": "2023070206",
	"dataseries": [
		{
			"cloudcover": 7,
			"temp2m": 21,
			"rh2m": "63%",
			"wind10m": {
			"direction": "W",
			"speed": 3
		},
		...
	]
}
````

<!-- element style="font-size: 12pt; margin: 0" -->

---

## API Use-Cases

* Frontend - Backend Communication
* Component Comunication
  * e.g. One Service calls another service
* DaaS

---

## Frontend-Backend

![API_Frontend_Backend](/software-engineering/web-development/assets/API_Frontend_Backend.excalidraw.svg)
Using APIs, you can link any software parts, potentially using different technologies, as long as they support the same protocol.

<!-- element style="font-size: 16pt" -->

---

## Component Communication

![API_Component_Communication](/software-engineering/web-development/assets/API_Component_Communication.excalidraw.svg)

---

## Protocols

<!-- element style="margin: 0" -->

There are several common protocols used in APIs.

<!-- element style="font-size: 20pt; margin: 0" -->

![API_Communication_Protocols](/software-engineering/web-development/assets/API_Communication_Protocols.excalidraw.svg)

<!-- element style="height: 600px; margin: 0" -->

---

## Attributes of protocols

**Protocols ...**

<!-- element style="text-align: left; width: 100vw" -->

* allow exchange of messages and information between 2 processes.
  * Processes don't need to be on the same physical machine. Can be distributed and communicate over network.
* are open to any technology or programming language.
* have a clear specification.
